title: Reflection & Garbage Collection
description: Explore Unreal's reflection system, UPROPERTY/UFUNCTION metadata, UCLASS specifiers, and generated header files.
learningObjectives: Explain how the Unreal Header Tool (UHT) parses source code variables
Use UPROPERTY and UFUNCTION specifiers to expose C++ variables to Blueprints
Identify the generated code linkage structures in generated.h headers

sections.title: Unreal reflection metadata system
sections.content:
C++ does not natively support reflection (the ability of a program to inspect its own types, classes, and properties at runtime). Unreal Engine builds a custom reflection system by running a pre-compile parse tool called the Unreal Header Tool (UHT). 

UHT parses macro definitions (UCLASS(), UPROPERTY(), UFUNCTION(), USTRUCT()) and writes reflection glue code in helper files (e.g. `MyClass.generated.h`), allowing the editor to display C++ variables, bind Blueprint pins, and perform garbage collection sweeps.

sections.title: Exposing variables to Blueprint graphs
